title: Ood Bnar
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Bnar}}

}}
{{Quote|<nowiki />''Curse'' you Jedi—I don't care if you're a ''million'' years old!|Sedriss|audio=Sedriss-CurseYou.flac|<ref name="Dark Empire II (audio)">[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Ood Bnar''' was a [[Neti/Legends|Neti]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived for over five thousand years, from before the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] until [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. Bnar was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ryyk]], but found himself stranded on [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] during his youth. He eventually joined the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], and over time became one of the most respected and revered Masters of the era. Bnar specialized in the history of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and preferred to avoid combat where possible, though he often advised more martial Jedi on how best to fight the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Eventually, he had such knowledge of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and Sith lore that he recorded a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]], which was passed down through the generations for centuries.

Bnar eventually became the understudy of Head Librarian [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] at the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], and after his friend's death at the hands of [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] in [[3996 BBY]], Bnar took over his role as Head Librarian. However, his reign would not last long. He was among the Jedi who discovered that they would have little time to evacuate before Ossus was destroyed by a Sith-created [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] in the nearby [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]]. While the other Jedi hurried to save as much as they could, Bnar went to his bundle of ancient [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], hoping they would survive the shockwave heading for the planet. However, he was interrupted by Exar Kun, who had come to Ossus to scavenge any artifacts left behind. Knowing he could not defeat the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Bnar transformed into a giant and immovable tree, barring Kun from the trove of sabers.

Bnar survived the devastation of Ossus, and entered a millennia-long slumber. He became known as the '''place of Ascension''' to the native [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]] tribe who for 4,000 years had performed the [[ritual of ascent]] there, believing that he was the source of [[The Force/Legends|Ysanna magic]]. He was awakened in 10 ABY to find [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kam Solusar]] facing off against a [[Dark Side Elite]] named [[Sedriss QL]], who had taken a [[Jem Ysanna|native girl]] hostage. Bnar freed the girl and gripped Sedriss tightly with his roots. The [[darksider]] attempted to use the dark energies contained in Ossus's skies against Bnar, but the Neti fought back, counteracting Sedriss's attack by using the powers rooted deep in the Ossus ground. He eventually sacrificed himself to stop Sedriss and save the two Jedi and the native.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|The gatekeeper of this teaching device is of a race of beings that evolved from the trees on the planet Myrkr.|Thon speaks of Ood Bnar|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi 5">[[Tales of the Jedi 5|''Tales of the Jedi'' 5]]</ref>}}
Ood Bnar was a member of the [[Neti/Legends|Neti]], a little-known tree-like [[Species/Legends|species]] with the ability to shape and change their bodies at will. He began life as a tiny seedling on the planet [[Ryyk]],<ref name="UAA" /> though he soon found himself orphaned and marooned on [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]].<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" /> He lived on the planet for his youth,<ref name="EGTPM">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref> which, given Neti's long lifespans, could have lasted over a century. Ood Bnar was [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]], and several centuries before the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|fall]] of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], he was found by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and began training as a Jedi apprentice.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />

===Jedi Master===
{{Quote|Greetings Jedi. I am Master Ood Bnar at your service.|Ood Bnar, Jedi Master|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi 5" />}}
Bnar excelled at his training, and eventually became a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Bnar was a revered [[Jedi historian|Jedi Scholar]], and took a particular interest in studying the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], though throughout his long career, Bnar was never seduced by the dark side. He possessed a rare ability in that he could completely sever himself from the subject of his studies; this strange capability allowed him to become one of the few Force-users to learn the workings of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and the dark side without falling under its sway. Ood Bnar became a revered repository of Jedi lore after centuries of study, and the Jedi Order began to rely on this vast knowledge whenever they fought the dark side themselves. He was a poor tactician himself, but he was able to inform his allies of their enemies' weaknesses and the best methods to exploit them, thus he indirectly helped resolve many crises during his tenure as a Jedi Master.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />

[[File:OodBnar_prophecy.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Ood Bnar's image as displayed by [[Jedi holocron|his holocron]].]]

Bnar did not take part in the Great Hyperspace War of [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], though one young [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], named [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]], did. Bnar and Odan-Urr, both extremely long-lived individuals, became close friends, and often instructed young Jedi together. Like Bnar, Odan-Urr was a Jedi Scholar, and after the war he built a [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]] on the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] in remembrance of his late Master [[Ooroo/Legends|Ooroo]]. The Library contained a plethora of Jedi lore, as well as the [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] Odan-Urr had recovered in the aftermath of the Great Hyperspace War.<ref name="POTJ">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> Odan-Urr was made Head Librarian and the [[Keeper of Antiquities]], while Bnar acted as his understudy.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

Over time, the other Jedi encouraged Bnar to construct a Jedi [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]], so that his knowledge might be kept for ever more. Aged one thousand years, he made a [[Jedi holocron|holocron]], containing a great amount of Jedi lore, as well as a comprehensive history of the Sith. The Neti Master installed a mimicry of himself to act as the [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeeper]] for the holocron. He gave it to one of his fellow Jedi, who passed it down through the generations, until it came into the possession of Master [[Arca Jeth]]. Jeth then loaned it to [[Thon]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], who was a close friend of Master Bnar.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />

[[File:OodWithShaela.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Bnar gives Shaela Nuur his rare Solari crystal.]]

Some time before [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], Bnar took a young [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] named [[Shaela Nuur]] as his apprentice. He taught her several aspects of being a Jedi, including [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] construction. Nuur excelled at her studies, and Bnar became impressed at how quickly she was progressing. He knew she would never be seduced by the dark side of the Force, and decided to give her his sacred [[Solari crystal/Legends|Solari]] crystal. The Neti presented it to her in the gardens on Ossus after she had completed building her lightsaber, though Nuur was shocked, thinking herself unworthy to hold the powerful focusing crystal. Bnar, however, knew that she was worthy, and told her that she would go on to do great things. Her training eventually finished, leaving Bnar student-less for the remainder of his time as a Jedi Master.<ref name="SL" />

Approximately 4,000 years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Bnar's homeworld, Ryyk, was destroyed by a cataclysmic [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]], which wiped out much of the species. Bnar was offworld teaching students on Ossus when this happened, so he survived, but nearly all of the Neti species was wiped out. Only fragments of their culture remained, and from that point onward information about the Neti was extremely scarce.<ref name="EGAS">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref>

===Conclave on Deneba===
{{Quote|The Jedi Masters have offered their wisdom. The rest is up to you. Now go.|Ood Bnar to Ulic Qel-Droma.|<ref name="TotJ Dark Lords of the Sith">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith]]''</ref>}}
In [[3997 BBY]], Ood Bnar was one of thousands of Jedi to attend a [[Conclave at Deneba|great convocation]] of the Jedi Order on the planet [[Deneba/Legends|Deneba]], called by Head Librarian Odan-Urr. Odan-Urr was growing worried with the recent attacks on the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by a dark side sect calling themselves the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]], and the ineptitude at dealing with them thus far displayed by the Republic forces. The Krath were led by two cousins, named [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]]—members of the [[Empress Teta/Legends|Tetan]] aristocracy, they had traveled to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], a planet known for its traditional allegiance to the dark side, and learned some basic dark side talents. The growing cult had already fought off a Republic/Jedi task force, largely thanks to Keto's powers, and had then arrogantly declared war on the Jedi Knights. Odan-Urr called forward [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and [[Nomi Sunrider]], the two leaders of the botched Republic attack, who had formulated a plan of attack which they hoped would stamp out the Krath. However, Qel-Droma's idea was not received well by the congregation. The young Human planned to infiltrate the Krath by pretending he had gone over to the dark side, and from there he would destroy them from within. The other Jedi, both the youthful and the experienced, warned Qel-Droma against it, knowing he would truly fall to the dark side should he attempt his plan.<ref name="Dark 3">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]]</ref>

[[File:OodHead.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Ood Bnar, Neti Jedi Master]]

Meanwhile, the Krath were plotting against the Jedi on Deneba. A [[Tetan Corsair]] lay hidden among the other vessels above Deneba, and released a barrage of [[Escape pod/Legends|life pods]], each containing hundreds of freshly produced [[Krath war droid/Legends|war droids]], down onto the mountainous world. Simultaneously, the service [[Droid/Legends|droids]] assigned to aid the Jedi turned on them, and a full-scale battle ensued. The droids were programmed to take out the most high-ranking and senior Jedi present. Though Bnar was not harmed, the same could not be said of all the Jedi Masters. Arca Jeth was killed by a war droid in the presence of his Padawan, Ulic Qel-Droma. The Jedi eventually defeated the Krath droids with minimal injuries, though Qel-Droma, feeling responsible for his Master's death, decided to carry out his plan regardless of the Jedi's attempts to dissuade him.<ref name="Dark 3" />

However, Bnar was determined not to see the Jedi fall to the dark side, and once again attempted to persuade him against carrying out his plan. In the Great Jedi Library on Ossus, Bnar and his old friend Odan-Urr met with Qel-Droma and his fellow Jedi Knight, Nomi Sunrider. The ancient Neti gave Qel-Droma a centuries-old [[Definitive history of the Empress Teta system|book]], authored by the influential [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]], which he hoped would prove useful during Qel-Droma's time in the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]]. Bnar knew Qel-Droma would not be swayed, but he introduced him to Odan-Urr regardless. Using the Sith holocron he had recovered over a thousand years beforehand, Odan-Urr demonstrated to him what had happened to a young Jedi who tried something similar to what Qel-Droma proposed—he fell to the dark side and murdered his Master. Qel-Droma was horrified at the thought of it, though he was still determined to carry out his plan, thinking he would become a hero. The wise Masters knew he would fail, and bade the two Jedi Knights goodbye, wishing the Force to be with them.<ref name="Dark 4" />

===Great Sith War===
{{Quote|Sometimes entities who have been initiated to the dark side can seduce a Jedi into giving way to the power of anger. Once rage is unleashed, it is difficult to control. It leads one farther and farther from the power of light. It even destroys ordinary common sense!|Ood Bnar speaks to the Jedi Knights about Ulic Qel-Droma's fall to the dark side at the hands of Krath sorceress Aleema Keto.|<ref name="TotJ Dark Lords of the Sith" />}}
Qel-Droma's efforts ultimately failed. He became seduced by Aleema Keto and her dark magic, and turned on his fellow Jedi. A team of Jedi Knights, including the likes of Nomi Sunrider, Qel-Droma's brother [[Cay Qel-Droma|Cay]], [[Qrrrl Toq]] and [[Dace Diath]], among others, had decided to travel to the Empress Teta system in an attempt to bring Ulic Qel-Droma back to the light. Bnar was one of several Jedi Masters who spoke to the team shortly prior to their departure. Master Thon told them that Qel-Droma might teach himself a lesson through his own foolish actions, but Bnar disagreed. He told the Knights that one who had turned to the dark side, such as Aleema Keto, could make a Jedi feel angry, and that rage clouded one's judgment and prevented the use of common sense. The Jedi Knights departed, though they soon returned, their mission a failure: Qel-Droma had refused to join them, telling them his true allegiance. Another impressionable young Jedi, a Human named [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], had also fallen to the dark side. The [[Force ghost/Legends|Sith spirit]] of [[Marka Ragnos]] proclaimed Kun the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], with Qel-Droma acting as his Sith apprentice.<ref name="Dark 6">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]]</ref> Along with the armies of [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] and the Krath, Kun waged war on the Republic. The first major casualty of the war was Odan-Urr, who was slaughtered by Kun on Ossus after the Dark Lord learned of his Sith holocron.<ref name="Sith War 1">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]]</ref> With his longtime friend dead, Ood Bnar became the new Head Librarian and Keeper of the Antiquities at the Great Jedi Library.<ref name="NEC" /> However, neither Bnar nor any of the other Jedi learned the truth behind Odan-Urr's death; Kun had lied, claiming that Odan-Urr had declared him a Jedi Master before succumbing to his age and becoming one with the Force.<ref name="Sith War 1" />

[[File:OodBnar-TSW5.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Ood Bnar during the Great Sith War.]]
Kun had secretly recruited several young Jedi and turned them to the dark side without the Order knowing.<ref name="Sith War 2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]]</ref> He had his acolytes kill their Jedi Masters, while he would take out his former Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] himself. Baas was killed by Kun on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="Sith War 3">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]]</ref> while Kun's [[Dark Jedi/Legends|dark Jedi]] succeeded in killing off their Masters, save for [[Oss Wilum]] and [[Crado]], who had been unable to defeat Master Thon on Ambria. Along with Nomi Sunrider and [[Sylvar]], Thon traveled to Ossus to assess the damage caused. They met with Bnar in a chamber off the Great Jedi Library, and were joined by a group of several [[Jedi Shadow]]s, who had been among the first to discover the bodies of the slaughtered Masters. The Jedi were discussing Qel-Droma's fall, and the eight Jedi Masters who had perished, when a young [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Jedi Knight named [[Tott Doneeta]] rushed in with news that the nearby [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[Kemplex Nine]], located in the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] of the [[Auril sector/Legends|Auril sector]], was being attacked by the Sith.<ref name="Sith War 4">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]]</ref>

The Jedi discussed what to do, with Thon advising caution; the station was unimportant by itself, and Kun had no reason to target it. He suspected it was a trap, though three of the Jedi Shadows—Qrrrl Toq, [[Shoaneb Culu]], and Dace Diath—would engage the Sith fleet and attempt to rescue those on the space station, while the others would remain on Ossus to ensure Kun could not launch a surprise assault. Thon guessed Kun's strategy correctly; the attack on Kemplex was nothing more than a ruse, though one which would have devastating effects for Ossus. Qel-Droma and Kun had ceased to require the services of Aleema Keto and Crado, and the two acolytes were beginning to get in the way of the two Sith's operations. Kun sent them on a suicide mission to the Cron Cluster, intending for the Republic and Jedi to give chase. They did, and when Keto was within reach of one of the Cluster's stars, she used her Sith magic to make it go supernova, destroying the Jedi and Republic forces sent after them. However, the supernova proved far more powerful than Keto had anticipated, and it killed her and Crado, as well as unleashing a shockwave that would devastate Ossus in very little time.<ref name="Sith War 4" />

===Devastation of Ossus===
{{Quote|Do not grieve for me, Thon. This is my metamorphosis, my life cycle. I have shielded my lightsaber treasures… I have defeated Exar Kun… I am satisfied. And perhaps, my friends, Ossus will protect me… enough.|Ood Bnar to Thon shortly before the devastation of Ossus|<ref name="Sith War 6">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 6]]</ref>}}
[[File:Transformation_of_ood.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Ood Bnar transforms.]]

While the other Jedi rushed to save as much of Odan-Urr's vast collection of scrolls as they possibly could, Bnar went to a private alcove in the Jedi archives, where he hid several ancient lightsabers in a concealed hole under the tiled floor. As he packed them away, Exar Kun entered, accompanied by several hulking [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors. Kun was trying to scavenge any artifacts left behind by the Jedi before they were lost forever, though Bnar was determined to stop the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. Kun ironically thanked the Neti Master for saving the artifacts for him, though Bnar did not waver, and ignited one of the ancient sabers, ready to face Kun.<ref name="Sith War 5">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 5]]</ref>

However, Kun had two large advantages over the old Neti Master: he had a unique [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]], and his skills with it were far better than Bnar's basic dueling abilities. However, the Neti held Kun off for several minutes, managing to evade his blows. Eventually, one of Exar Kun's Massassi stepped in, forcefully knocking the saber from Bnar's branch-like hands. On his knees and defenseless, Bnar knew he would not overcome the Dark Lord of the Sith, yet he was determined to keep the sabers out of the man's hands. Using his Neti abilities, he metamorphosed into his true, tree-like state, standing to protect a trove of sacred lightsabers from Kun. The Sith Lord left, knowing that even without the lightsabers, he was victorious.<ref name="Sith War 5" />

Kun fled to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], while Qel-Droma was [[Sever Force/Legends|cut off from the Force]] by Nomi Sunrider after killing his own brother. Thon and the others had managed to save some of the scrolls and documents Odan-Urr had kept in his library, though they remarked that even if they had had a year to pack away the scrolls, they would still have had to leave some behind. Before he left, Thon went to visit Ood Bnar, his old friend. Bnar was unable to move, though he could still talk and relate to other beings. The [[Tchuukthai]] Master could not fathom Bnar's actions, yet he accepted them, and was saddened to have lost his friend in such a way. Bnar told him not to be concerned; this was his life-cycle, and he may even survive the cataclysm of Ossus. Thon wished him well, and the two said goodbye, never to see each other again.<ref name="Sith War 6" /> Bnar also left his [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]], which would be later called [[Bnar's Sacrifice]], as a legacy to those who would come after him.<ref name="Card-SWGTCG-Bnar's Sacrifice">{{SWGTCG|set=Agents of Deception|cardname=Bnar's Sacrifice}}</ref> His exploits in this battle were later portrayed in an artwork by the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] master artist [[Zyk-yadoon]] that was kept at the [[Remembrance Hall]] on [[Almas]].<ref name="Almas, Sweet Almas: Desert Cries">{{LivingForce|Almas, Sweet Almas: Desert Cries}}</ref>

===Reawakening===
{{Quote|I am Ood…it has been a looong time since I sensed the presence of another Jedi!|Ood Bnar, after awakening.|<ref name="Dark Empire II">''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]''</ref>}}
The shockwave hit Ossus soon after Thon's departure, though as he had foreseen, Bnar managed to survive the devastation.<ref name="Sith War 6" /> Ossus, however, was ruined, and the intense radiation meant that the Jedi were unable to rebuild their libraries and training centers there. Bnar was not the only survivor—a group of Jedi unable to evacuate had found refuge in great caverns under the [[Eocho Mountains/Legends|Eocho Mountains]], where their descendants lived until they had forgotten their origins. They eventually became the tribal [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]].<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> Bnar was never discovered by the Jedi of his time, and over time his friends perished. Thon saw out the rest of his days on Ambria,<ref name="POTJ" /> while Shaela Nuur was killed during the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]] shortly after the war.<ref name="SL" /> Bnar eventually entered a centuries-long slumber, living through many eras of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], where the majority of Jedi Order was wiped out, minus a few survivors. He was not forgotten, however: [[Bodo Baas]], a descendant of Bnar's friend Vodo-Siosk Baas, spoke of the Neti Master in his [[Jedi holocron|holocron]], which would eventually fall into the hands of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a young Human from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] who sought to rebuild the Jedi Order.<ref name="DEII 3" /> Bnar eventually became known as the "place of Ascension" to the native Ysanna, who came to believe that he was the source of Ysanna magic. For 4,000 years, the Ysanna had performed the [[ritual of ascent]] by tying individuals to him to imbue them with magic.<ref name="Dark Empire II (audio)" />

[[File:OodBnarSacrifice-DEII3.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The death of Ood Bnar.]]

In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], over four millennia after he had metamorphosed, Ood Bnar was awakened. Luke Skywalker and [[Kam Solusar]] had been guided to Ossus by Bodo Baas's holocron, and found two of the native Ysanna, named [[Jem Ysanna|Jem]] and [[Rayf Ysanna|Rayf]], tied to what appeared to be a large and gnarled tree. They released the pair and were introduced to the Ysanna tribe, who allied themselves with Skywalker and Solusar upon learning that they were Jedi. Skywalker had been followed, however, by two of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Dark Side Elite]]: [[Sedriss QL]] and [[Vill Goir]], who had brought a contingent of troops with them as backup. They engaged the Ysanna and Jedi in a battle, though their soldiers were fought off, and Goir was killed by Kam Solusar. Alone against two powerful Jedi, Sedriss took Jem hostage and backed up against the momentous tree form of Ood Bnar.<ref name="DEII 3" />

The presence of so many powerful Force-users, particularly that of the Jedi, stirred Ood Bnar out of his sleep. He used his root-like arms to grab a hold of Sedriss, and pulled him away from the frightened Ysanna female. Sensing that it would be his final battle, Sedriss attempted to use the energy of the planet's broken skies to defeat Bnar. Bnar, however, was able to counteract this, and drew on the [[Beam of light|living energy]] present in his roots deep in the Ossus ground. In the end, the two Force-users annihilated each other. The Jedi were saddened that Bnar was unable to share his knowledge with them, though his trove of ancient lightsabers helped in the [[New Jedi Order|restoration]] of the Jedi Order. Bnar was now one with the Force, however, before his death he was successfully able to leave a [[Ood Bnar's descendant|descendant]] behind: a tiny sapling that grew upon his death.<ref name="DEII 3" />

==Legacy==
Long after the end of the Great Sith War, treasure hunters scoured the galaxy for [[Journals of Ood Bnar|journals]] presumably written by Ood Bnar himself. Although their existence was debated by the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the explorer [[Riyan Kiner]] became utterly insane after spending his life looking for this fabled artifact.<ref name="SWG:TMHP1">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|The Meatlumps Hideout, Part 1|Corellia}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Ood_Bnar.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Master Bnar]]
{{Quote|These lightsabers belong to the Jedi…I will protect them with my life if I must!|Ood Bnar shows his selflessness|<ref name="Sith War 5" />}}
Ood Bnar was a wise individual, and his word was highly regarded within the Jedi Order. His centuries studying both the light and the dark meant that he was extremely knowledgeable, and the other Jedi felt his knowledge and expertise were so important that they should be recorded in a holocron.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" /> Students were often sent to Master Bnar so that they could learn a lesson; his experience of Jedi lore meant he could often determine what would happen should one attempt something, as it had nearly always been attempted previously.<ref name="Dark 4" /> Some of Bnar's lectures on the Force were even published by the Coruscant Jedi Temple, in a collection named "''[[Lectures on the Nature of the Force]]''."<ref name="Life's Memories article">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/swlf20030703memories|text=Life's Memories}}</ref>

Throughout his life, Bnar made many friends, despite knowing that he would live to see the death of nearly all of them. He and Thon were extremely close,<ref name="Sith War 6" /> although they disagreed on many subjects. He also conferred with the other Masters often, including Vodo-Siosk Baas, one of the most senior Masters. His closest friend was Odan-Urr, with whom he maintained the Great Jedi Library on Ossus. He and Odan-Urr often consulted people together, and Bnar had great respect for the [[Draethos/Legends|Draethos]], considering him one of the wisest of the Masters.<ref name="Dark 4" /> Bnar took many students, and he was known to be an encouraging teacher, offering advice where he could, but also praising a promising student's abilities. His favorite apprentice was Shaela Nuur, whom he kindly awarded his rare Solari crystal to in acknowledgment of her steady progress.<ref name="SL" /> Although Bnar had no qualms with teaching other Jedi of the dark side, he always warned them of the perils the dark side possessed, and that they should think carefully before deciding to learn of it.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />

Bnar was a very quiet and peaceful individual, preferring to stay out of the limelight during times of crisis.<ref name="Dark 4" /><ref name="Sith War 4" /> He spent much time alone, though this did not bother him. He disliked violence, and only learned basic skills with a lightsaber.<ref name="Sith War 5" /> Although he preferred to remain in the background most of the time, Bnar was happy to speak in front of others, and often attended conferences with other members of the Order.<ref name="Dark 6" /><ref name="Sith War 4" /> Bnar discouraged the thought that Humans were in any way superior to other species, and also felt that every creature, even non-sentients, were as important as each other. He thought that the Force linked everything, so everything was of equal importance.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />

A very studious and scholarly character, Bnar held an extremely rare ability: he was able to distance himself when learning the various aspects of the Force, particularly the dark side, so he was never tempted to renounce the light and worship the dark in his millennia-long life. This way of being proved instrumental during Bnar's lifetime, as he was one of very few ''pure'' Jedi to have an intricate knowledge of the dark side, so he could always advise more martial Jedi on where its weaknesses were and how to combat them.<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref> Ood Bnar was extremely selfless, and was happy to sacrifice himself for the good of others. During the Great Sith War, he metamorphosed to save the lightsabers from Kun's hands;<ref name="Sith War 6" /> though the transformation was inevitable for a Neti, Bnar could have remained in his previous state for much longer.<ref name="UAA" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|This evil one is trying to kill me! But I am a greater master of the Force than he.|Ood Bnar confronts Sedriss QL|<ref name="DEII 3" />}}
[[File:OodVsExar.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Ood Bnar duels with Exar Kun.]]

Bnar was an accomplished Jedi, though he preferred reading to fighting, and his lightsaber skills could be found lacking when facing faster and superior opponents. Nevertheless, he could wield a weapon decently, though he did not carry a lightsaber himself.<ref name="Sith War 5" /> Shaela Nuur, Ood's Padawan, was a good swordsman, which was a testament to Bnar's abilities.<ref name="SL" /> However, Bnar knew his limitations, and tried to avoid one-on-one lightsaber fights whenever he could.<ref name="Sith War 5" />

Although Bnar was not a master swordsman, he was an expert at using the Force, and had a wide array of powers in his repertoire. He was able to draw on the energies held deep below Ossus's surface and use them against Sedriss QL, who was attempting something similar, though the [[darksider]] was trying to use the dark energies contained in Ossus's atmosphere against Bnar. The Neti Jedi proved equally powerful, and the two Force-wielders ultimately destroyed each other.<ref name="DEII 3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ood Bnar was conceived by ''[[Star Wars]]'' author [[Tom Veitch]] for the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Saga of Nomi Sunrider|''Saga of Nomi Sunrider'']] story arc, part of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] comic series. His visual appearance was designed by artist [[Chris Gossett]].<ref name="interview">[[Wookieepedia:Interview/Christian Gossett|Interview with Christian Gossett]] with [[Chris Gossett]]</ref> Veitch later re-used the character in the subsequent ''Tales of the Jedi'' story-arc, as well as in ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]''.

Ood Bnar acts as the in-universe "author" of the ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', where he acts as the gatekeeper of the holocron, and instructs players on various aspects of running a ''Tales of the Jedi'' [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|RPG]] campaign.

Ood Bnar makes an appearance in the Jedi Under Siege expansion for Star Wars: The Old Republic, in his Tree form, a codex entry states that Jedi Master [[Gnost-Dural]] along with other Jedi meditated at his roots in hopes of learning from the ancient master, to no avail. Due to the visual differences between his final resting place in Dark Empire and the lack of reply to the Jedi with Gnost-Dural, this may not have been him, but a seedling similar to [[Ood Bnar's descendant]].
